card.title: mazer: run-quality metric contract v2
card.summary: Define and implement one versioned, topology-aware run-quality contract before downstream score, rank, leaderboard, achievement, or player-facing adoption.
card.objective: Keep runtime, progression, reports, audits, calibration, and future receipts correlated to playable wrap-aware shortest-path truth while preserving historical stored-versus-recomputed boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Every metric has a deterministic numerator, denominator, version, and undefined-state policy.
Equally short valid routes score equivalently and legal edge wrap remains playable graph movement.
Clean Run and Explorer remain compatible with historical immutability and later UI adoption.
Public rank and player-facing score remain unchanged until separate adoption cards are admitted.
discoveries: Shortest-path corridor fidelity must use the union of every equally short route, not one generator solution path.
Complete topology aggregates must be computed before bounded path storage; previews cannot support truthful reconstruction.
Legacy single-solution wrong-turn counters cannot disqualify an otherwise legal alternate or wrap clean run.
nextActions: Review and merge Mazer draft PR #73 if the private/internal total and telemetry-only topology contract are accepted.
After merge, admit downstream capture and player-results cards without redefining the v1 denominators.
Keep historical receipts immutable and never synthesize missing topology metrics from previews.
evidence: Full serial Mazer verification: 51/51 files and 370/370 tests passed
TypeScript no-emit, production bundle build, and five-seed deterministic calibration passed

entry.headline: Versioned run-quality and topology contract is review-ready
completed: Unified runtime, progression, reporting, audit, calibration, and receipt score authority.
Defined optimal/accepted steps, coverage, corridor union fidelity, exploration, revisits, Clean Run, Explorer, and undefined policies.
Proved equal-route parity, legal wrap, reset, incomplete run, bounded storage, and historical immutability behavior.
discovered: Topology metrics are private telemetry in this packet; downstream public score/rank adoption remains separately governed.
next: Review PR #73, then merge or return focused contract feedback.
evidence: 54/54 focused and 370/370 full serial tests passed
Build, type check, calibration, and diff check passed
